definition_population.content: Chronic relapsing-remitting inflammatory bowel disease affecting colonic mucosa continuously from the rectum; bimodal age of onset.

epidemiology.content: Global IBD incidence/prevalence systematically reviewed across population-based studies since 1990; no single quotable UC-only numeric figure retrieved in this pass.

sources.quote: Inflammatory bowel disease is a global disease in the 21st century. We aimed to assess the changing incidence and prevalence of inflammatory bowel disease around the world.

biology.content: ERN1 encodes IRE1, a UPR sensor implicated in UC epithelial ER stress and intestinal inflammation. IRE1-XBP1 signaling also directly regulates T-cell mitochondrial activity/effector function in other disease contexts, suggesting a plausible T-cell-relevant mechanism, though not UC-specific in the retrieved literature. Preclinical MSC therapy modulates this axis in UC.

sources.quote: This state induces a response (unfolded protein response (UPR)), initiated by the activation of IRE1/Xbp-1, PERK/eIF2, and ATF6 pathways, which has previously been linked to intestinal inflammation in experimental models.
activates the IRE1α-XBP1 arm of the unfolded protein response in T cells to control their mitochondrial respiration and anti-tumour function
Human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells alleviate colon epithelial apoptosis in ulcerative colitis by activating inositol-requiring enzyme 1/X-box binding protein 1 signaling

treatment.content: Standard UC therapy: 5-ASAs, corticosteroids, thiopurines, biologics (anti-TNF/integrin/IL-12,23), JAK inhibitors. No ERN1/IRE1-targeted approved therapy identified.

regulatory_trials.content: 1,290 total interventional trials for UC on ClinicalTrials.gov. Example: completed Phase 2b JAK inhibitor dose-ranging trial NCT02958865.

sources.quote: A PHASE 2B, DOUBLE-BLIND, RANDOMIZED, PLACEBO-CONTROLLED, PARALLEL GROUP, DOSE RANGING STUDY OF ORAL PF-06651600 AND PF-06700841 AS INDUCTION AND CHRONIC THERAPY IN SUBJECTS WITH MODERATE TO SEVERE ULCERATIVE COLITIS

gaps: No single quotable numeric UC-only prevalence figure retrieved.
No UC-specific (as opposed to general UPR/other-disease) study directly linking ERN1 to CD4+ T-cell function was found.
